odeProperty.value: Learning situation corresponding to Unit 7 of 1st year of ESO, carried out in April 2026.

odeComponent.htmlView:
Hello, explorers! In the next two weeks, we are going to travel in time. We will learn about great explorers and their discoveries. But we are not going to read a boring book... You are going to be the creators!

What is our mission? At the end of this journey, you will work in pairs to create two big projects:

  • An Interactive Infographic about a famous explorer (like Amelia Earhart, Ibn Battuta, or Edmund Hillary).
  • A "Time Travel" Podcast: A radio interview.

Are you ready for the adventure? Let's go!

What is our mission? At the end of this journey, you will work in pairs to create two big projects:

odeComponent.htmlView:
Grammar: Our Explorer's Tools

To talk about the great explorers of the history, we need two special tools in our backpack. Let's review them!

1. Past Simple: Verb To be

We use the past of the verb to be to talk about situations in the past or to say when someone was born.

Affirmative and Negative:

I, He, She, It ➡️ was / wasn't (She was born in England. He wasn't at school.)
You, We, They ➡️ were / weren't (They were excited. We weren't invited.)
Questions:

Yes/No Questions: Was he surprised? (Yes, he was / No, he wasn't). Were they bored? (Yes, they were).
Wh- Questions: Where were you yesterday? Who was a teacher?

2. Adjectives: -ed vs -ing

🚨⚠️🚨Adjectives can end in -ing or -ed, but they mean different things!🚨⚠️🚨 

Use -ed for Feelings: It describes how a person feels inside.

Example: They were so frightened! (¡Estaban muy asustados!)

Use -ing for Things or Situations: It describes the thing that causes the feeling.

Example: This film is frightening! (¡Esta película es aterradora!)

 

(Note: If you say "I'm boring", it means "soy una persona aburrida". If you say "I'm bored", it means "estoy aburrido/a").

odeComponent.htmlView:
📖 Dictionary of Exploration

Here is the vocabulary you need for your missions. Learn these words to be a great journalist and explorer!

🌍🧭🌍TRAVELLING (Viajes y lugares): 

Countries / Places: Países / Lugares.
Culture / History: Cultura / Historia.
Dangerous: Peligroso/a.
Maps: Mapas.
Travel / Trip / Journey: Viajar / Viaje corto / Travesía.
Traveller: Viajero/a.


Adjectives for Famous Explorers:                                                                                      

Brave / Courageous / Daring: Valiente / Atrevido/a.
Conquer: Conquistar.
Discover: Descubrir.
Early life: Primeros años (infancia).
Expedition: Expedición.
Pioneer: Pionero/a (the first person to do something).
Tribute: Homenaje.


General adjectives

Bored / Boring: Aburrido/a (feeling) / Aburrido/a (thing).
Excited / Exciting: Emocionado/a / Emocionante.
Fascinated / Fascinating: Fascinado/a / Fascinante.
Frightened / Frightening: Asustado/a / Aterrador o que da miedo.
Interested / Interesting: Interesado/a / Interesante.
Surprised / Surprising: Sorprendido/a / Sorprendente.
Terrified / Terrifying: Aterrorizado/a / Espeluznante.

odeComponent.htmlView:
Let's do it! You are going to design a visual biography of your explorer.

Where?

- Use Canva or Genially.
What to include?

A free photo (Creative Commons) of your explorer.
At least three interactive buttons with interesting facts.
Short texts using was/were and adjectives (-ed/-ing).

How to send it? Finish your design, copy the public link, and paste it in Microsoft Teams (General Channel > Assignments > Mission 1).

%7B%22typeGame%22%3A%22Rubric%22%2C%22version%22%3A2%2C%22id%22%3A%22idevice-1778002909212-mpx0khp2a%22%2C%22table%22%3A%7B%22title%22%3A%22INFOGRAPHIC%20RUBRIC%22%2C%22categories%22%3A%5B%22Grammar%20and%20Vocabulary%22%2C%22Interactivity%20and%20content%22%2C%22Visuals%20and%20Copyrightand%20Content%22%2C%22Format%20and%20Delivery%22%5D%2C%22scores%22%3A%5B%22Excellent%22%2C%22Good%22%2C%22Developing%22%2C%22Needs%20improvement%22%5D%2C%22descriptions%22%3A%5B%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%224%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Correctly%20uses%20was/were%20and%20-ed/-ing%20adjectives%20in%20short%20texts.%20No%20grammatical%20or%20spelling%20errors%2C%20or%20very%20minor%20ones%20that%20do%20not%20affect%20comprehension.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%223%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Uses%20was/were%20and%20-ed/-ing%20adjectives%2C%20but%20makes%20some%20grammatical%20or%20spelling%20errors%20that%20do%20not%20impede%20overall%20comprehension.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%222%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Attempts%20to%20use%20the%20required%20structures%2C%20but%20there%20are%20several%20serious%20errors%2C%20or%20omits%20one%20of%20the%20two%20key%20elements.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Does%20not%20use%20the%20required%20structures%2C%20or%20the%20texts%20are%20incomprehensible%20/%20directly%20copied%20from%20the%20internet.%22%7D%5D%2C%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%223%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Includes%203%20or%20more%20interactive%20buttons%20that%20work%20perfectly.%20The%20hidden%20facts%20are%20relevant%2C%20interesting%2C%20and%20show%20good%20research.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%222%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Includes%203%20interactive%20buttons%2C%20but%20the%20information%20is%20too%20brief%2C%20not%20very%20relevant%2C%20or%20there%20is%20a%20minor%20technical%20glitch.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Includes%20only%201%20or%202%20interactive%20buttons%2C%20or%20the%20information%20is%20not%20directly%20related%20to%20the%20explorer%27s%20biography.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%220.5%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Does%20not%20include%20interactive%20buttons%2C%20or%20none%20of%20them%20work%20when%20clicking%20on%20the%20submitted%20link.%22%7D%5D%2C%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%222%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Includes%20a%20photo%20of%20the%20explorer%20explicitly%20free%20of%20copyright%20%28Creative%20Commons%29.%20The%20visual%20design%20is%20clear%2C%20attractive%2C%20and%20easy%20to%20read.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221.75%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Descriptor%20%283.2%29%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221.50%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Descriptor%20%283.3%29%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221.25%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Descriptor%20%283.4%29%22%7D%5D%2C%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%221%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Uses%20Canva%20or%20Genially%20and%20successfully%20submits%20the%20public%20link%20in%20the%20indicated%20Teams%20channel%20and%20on%20time.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%220.75%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Uses%20Canva%20or%20Genially%2C%20but%20requires%20teacher%20assistance%20to%20make%20the%20link%20public%20or%20to%20find%20the%20right%20assignment%20section.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%220.50%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Submits%20the%20assignment%2C%20but%20the%20link%20is%20not%20public%20%28requires%20requesting%20access%29%20or%20uses%20a%20different%20format%20%28e.g.%2C%20PDF%29.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%220%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Does%20not%20submit%20the%20link%20in%20Microsoft%20Teams%20or%20does%20not%20use%20any%20digital%20tool%20to%20create%20the%20project.%22%7D%5D%5D%7D%2C%22title%22%3A%22INFOGRAPHIC%20RUBRIC%22%2C%22categories%22%3A%5B%22Grammar%20and%20Vocabulary%22%2C%22Interactivity%20and%20content%22%2C%22Visuals%20and%20Copyrightand%20Content%22%2C%22Format%20and%20Delivery%22%5D%2C%22scores%22%3A%5B%22Excellent%22%2C%22Good%22%2C%22Developing%22%2C%22Needs%20improvement%22%5D%2C%22descriptions%22%3A%5B%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%224%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Correctly%20uses%20was/were%20and%20-ed/-ing%20adjectives%20in%20short%20texts.%20No%20grammatical%20or%20spelling%20errors%2C%20or%20very%20minor%20ones%20that%20do%20not%20affect%20comprehension.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%223%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Uses%20was/were%20and%20-ed/-ing%20adjectives%2C%20but%20makes%20some%20grammatical%20or%20spelling%20errors%20that%20do%20not%20impede%20overall%20comprehension.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%222%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Attempts%20to%20use%20the%20required%20structures%2C%20but%20there%20are%20several%20serious%20errors%2C%20or%20omits%20one%20of%20the%20two%20key%20elements.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Does%20not%20use%20the%20required%20structures%2C%20or%20the%20texts%20are%20incomprehensible%20/%20directly%20copied%20from%20the%20internet.%22%7D%5D%2C%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%223%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Includes%203%20or%20more%20interactive%20buttons%20that%20work%20perfectly.%20The%20hidden%20facts%20are%20relevant%2C%20interesting%2C%20and%20show%20good%20research.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%222%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Includes%203%20interactive%20buttons%2C%20but%20the%20information%20is%20too%20brief%2C%20not%20very%20relevant%2C%20or%20there%20is%20a%20minor%20technical%20glitch.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Includes%20only%201%20or%202%20interactive%20buttons%2C%20or%20the%20information%20is%20not%20directly%20related%20to%20the%20explorer%27s%20biography.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%220.5%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Does%20not%20include%20interactive%20buttons%2C%20or%20none%20of%20them%20work%20when%20clicking%20on%20the%20submitted%20link.%22%7D%5D%2C%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%222%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Includes%20a%20photo%20of%20the%20explorer%20explicitly%20free%20of%20copyright%20%28Creative%20Commons%29.%20The%20visual%20design%20is%20clear%2C%20attractive%2C%20and%20easy%20to%20read.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221.75%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Descriptor%20%283.2%29%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221.50%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Descriptor%20%283.3%29%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221.25%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Descriptor%2 %3Cp%3EComplete%20the%20following%20rubric%3C/p%3E

Lights, microphone, action! 🎬

Welcome to your second mission. It is time to travel to the past and use your voice! You are going to record a short radio interview.

👥 How to work?

You will work in your cooperative pairs randomly chosen. You have two roles:

Student A (The Journalist): You work for National Geographic. You ask the questions.


Student B (The Explorer): You are the famous explorer from your Infographic. You answer the questions.

📝 Step 1: The Script (El guion)

Work together to write the interview.

Write 4 questions and 4 answers.
You must use the Past Simple (was/were) and Adjectives (-ed / -ing).
 

Example:

Journalist: Were you frightened in the expedition?
Explorer: Yes, I was terrified! The mountain was very dangerous.

🗣️ Step 2: Practice! (¡Ensaya!)

Practice your dialogue. Remember to use a loud and clear voice. You are actors!

🎧 Step 3: Recording and Editing (Grabación y edición)

  • Open the webpage Vocaroo on the school computer or Audacity.
  • Record your interview.
  • Extra challenge: Add a background sound effect (wind, ocean, jungle...) from Freesound.org to make it realistic. 

📤 Step 4: Submit your mission (Entrega)

When your podcast is ready, export the file as an .mp3.

Go to Microsoft Teams.
Upload your audio to: General Channel > Files > Podcasts 1º ESO.

Remember to name it including both your and your classmate's Name and Surname. As in the following example: MARÍA_ANTEQUERA_PLATAS_and_ULISES_RODRÍGUEZ_PÉREZ.mp3

🏆 How will this mission be evaluated? The teacher will use a Rubric to evaluate your podcast. We will check:

Grammar: Correct use of was/were and vocabulary.
Pronunciation: Clear voice and good intonation.
Creativity: Acting and sound effects.

Good luck, time travellers! ⏱️🚀

Lights, microphone, action! 🎬

Welcome to your second mission. It is time to travel to the past and use your voice! You are going to record a short radio interview.

👥 How to work?

You will work in your cooperative pairs randomly chosen. You have two roles:

Student A (The Journalist): You work for National Geographic. You ask the questions.


Student B (The Explorer): You are the famous explorer from your Infographic. You answer the questions.

📝 Step 1: The Script (El guion)

Work together to write the interview.

Write 4 questions and 4 answers.
You must use the Past Simple (was/were) and Adjectives (-ed / -ing).
 

Example:

Journalist: Were you frightened in the expedition?
Explorer: Yes, I was terrified! The mountain was very dangerous.

🗣️ Step 2: Practice! (¡Ensaya!)

Practice your dialogue. Remember to use a loud and clear voice. You are actors!

🎧 Step 3: Recording and Editing (Grabación y edición)

  • Open the webpage Vocaroo on the school computer or Audacity.
  • Record your interview.
  • Extra challenge: Add a background sound effect (wind, ocean, jungle...) from Freesound.org to make it realistic. 

📤 Step 4: Submit your mission (Entrega)

When your podcast is ready, export the file as an .mp3.

Go to Microsoft Teams.
Upload your audio to: General Channel > Files > Podcasts 1º ESO.

Remember to name it including both your and your classmate's Name and Surname. As in the following example: MARÍA_ANTEQUERA_PLATAS_and_ULISES_RODRÍGUEZ_PÉREZ.mp3

🏆 How will this mission be evaluated? The teacher will use a Rubric to evaluate your podcast. We will check:

Grammar: Correct use of was/were and vocabulary.
Pronunciation: Clear voice and good intonation.
Creativity: Acting and sound effects.

Good luck, time travellers! ⏱️🚀

%7B%22typeGame%22%3A%22Rubric%22%2C%22version%22%3A2%2C%22id%22%3A%22idevice-1777936727977-zqa2uuvzi%22%2C%22table%22%3A%7B%22title%22%3A%22A%20GREAT%20ADVENTURER%20RUBRIC%22%2C%22categories%22%3A%5B%221.%20Content%20%26%20Script%20%20%20%28Contenido%20y%20Guion%29%22%2C%22Criterios%202%22%2C%22Criterios%203%22%2C%22Criterios%204%22%5D%2C%22scores%22%3A%5B%22Expert%20Explorer%20%284%20pts%29%22%2C%22Great%20Adventurer%20%283%20pts%29%22%2C%22Beginner%20Traveller%20%282%20pts%29%22%2C%22Lost%20in%20Time%20%281%20pt%29%22%5D%2C%22descriptions%22%3A%5B%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%223%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22The%20interview%20has%204%20questions%20and%204%20answers.%20The%20information%20about%20the%20explorer%20is%20correct%20and%20very%20interesting.%20%281.1%29%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%222%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22The%20interview%20has%203%20or%204%20questions.%20The%20information%20about%20the%20explorer%20is%20good.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22The%20interview%20has%20only%202%20questions.%20Some%20information%20is%20incorrect%20or%20missing.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%220.50%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22The%20interview%20is%20very%20short%20%28only%201%20question%29%20or%20the%20information%20is%20not%20about%20the%20explorer.%22%7D%5D%2C%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%223%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Excellent%20use%20of%20was/were%2C%20adjectives%20%28-ed/-ing%29%20and%20exploration%20words.%20No%20mistakes%21%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%222%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Good%20use%20of%20was/were%20and%20adjectives.%20There%20are%20only%201%20or%202%20small%20mistakes.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Some%20problems%20with%20was/were%20or%20adjectives.%20There%20are%203%20or%204%20mistakes.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%220.50%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Many%20grammar%20mistakes.%20They%20don%27t%20use%20the%20vocabulary%20from%20the%20unit.%22%7D%5D%2C%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%222%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Very%20clear%20voice%20and%20excellent%20pronunciation.%20It%20sounds%20like%20a%20real%2C%20natural%20conversation.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221.50%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Clear%20voice%20and%20good%20pronunciation.%20Sometimes%20they%20read%20a%20little%20fast%20or%20slow.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Sometimes%20it%20is%20difficult%20to%20hear%20or%20understand%20the%20words.%20They%20are%20just%20reading.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%220.50%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22It%20is%20very%20difficult%20to%20understand%20the%20words.%20The%20volume%20is%20too%20low.%22%7D%5D%2C%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%222%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Excellent%20acting%20%28roleplay%29%21%20They%20use%20background%20sound%20effects%20%28wind%2C%20ocean...%29%20very%20well.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221.50%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22Good%20acting.%20They%20use%20a%20sound%20effect%2C%20but%20the%20volume%20is%20too%20loud%20or%20too%20quiet%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22They%20try%20to%20act%2C%20but%20there%20are%20no%20background%20sound%20effects.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%220.50%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22No%20acting%20%28reading%20like%20a%20robot%29%20and%20no%20sound%20effects.%22%7D%5D%5D%7D%2C%22title%22%3A%22A%20GREAT%20ADVENTURER%20RUBRIC%22%2C%22categories%22%3A%5B%221.%20Content%20%26%20Script%20%20%20%28Contenido%20y%20Guion%29%22%2C%22Criterios%202%22%2C%22Criterios%203%22%2C%22Criterios%204%22%5D%2C%22scores%22%3A%5B%22Expert%20Explorer%20%284%20pts%29%22%2C%22Great%20Adventurer%20%283%20pts%29%22%2C%22Beginner%20Traveller%20%282%20pts%29%22%2C%22Lost%20in%20Time%20%281%20pt%29%22%5D%2C%22descriptions%22%3A%5B%5B%7B%22weight%22%3A%223%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22The%20interview%20has%204%20questions%20and%204%20answers.%20The%20information%20about%20the%20explorer%20is%20correct%20and%20very%20interesting.%20%281.1%29%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%222%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22The%20interview%20has%203%20or%204%20questions.%20The%20information%20about%20the%20explorer%20is%20good.%22%7D%2C%7B%22weight%22%3A%221%22%2C%22text%22%3A%22The%20interview
